title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity · Graph Lens: Gita Object Graph Invariants
summary: A long-lived repository grows unexpectedly, contains unreachable objects, and reports a missing tree after interrupted maintenance; the team needs causal storage evidence before cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita object graph invariants increment in object-database integrity and reachability report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Object Graph Invariants
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G95 is a theory in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Object Graph Invariants contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.

content.text: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G95 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation. A defensible solution applies Gita Object Graph Invariants, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to object-database integrity and reachability report.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised
git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable
# expected: fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them
# observation: the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only
# changed-case: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation
# transfer-limit: quarantine and copy real object storage before repair because this simulator has no pack corruption
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Object Graph Invariants evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-object-graph-invariants-1pyrmv" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Object Graph Invariants into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-object-graph-invariants-1pyrmv
# scenario-key: trace-1pyrmv probe-vmryp1 boundary-yrmv1p owner-1pyrmvx fault-1pyrmvz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1p candidate=b00b1mv
# hypothesis: the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised
git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable
# expected: fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them
# observation: the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only
# changed-case: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable
# after-ref: main=a11ce1p candidate=c0ffeev
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation
# transfer-limit: quarantine and copy real object storage before repair because this simulator has no pack corruption
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
